title: galium leiophyllum var. subvelutinum
intro: Galium leiophyllum var. subvelutinum, also known as Prairie Bedstraw, is a perennial herb native to North America, characterized by its small white flowers and velvety stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Prairie Bedstraw include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the urinary system, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as urinary tract infections and kidney stones. Prairie Bedstraw can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional due to potential interactions and side effects.
